description: REVERSE-ENGINEERED from the implemented code (replaces the earlier forward-design spec). Stage 1 of the split newModule flow: it delivers ONLY the durable business model (the 'behavior contract') = ontology + rules + workflows + operations, plus the actor authorization roster and deterministic BFF naming handoff. It STOPS before screen/contract implementation (Stage 2) and backend persistence/controller implementation (Stage 3), which start as SEPARATE tasks that read only the frozen l4 artifacts.

source.schemas: _102020_/l2/agentNewSolution2/ns2Schemas.ts (strict JSON schemas; collab-llm enforces, ns2Extract re-validates)

conventions.selfContained: Everything lives in l2/agentNewSolution2/. No imports from the legacy agentNewSolution (which will be deleted).
conventions.agentNaming: Regenerated reused agents are renamed agentNs2* (clean, focused prompts). The four NEW agents keep their plain names. Containers are agentNewSolution2*.
conventions.layerPlacement: l4 = BUSINESS. module map + ontology are module-scoped (l4/{module}); rules/workflows/operations/actors are GLOBAL (may span modules). l5 = project data only.

artifactPaths.moduleStructural: l4/{module}/module.defs.ts  (module meta, designContext [initial prompt + userLanguage + openDetails + priority decisions, for Stage 2], ontology index [entityId -> defPath], relationships, approvedArtifacts). NO top-level capabilities (realized — with priority — on each workflow/operation). NO actors (see actors).
artifactPaths.ontologyEntity: l4/{module}/ontology/{EntityId}.defs.ts  (canonical entity: fields, per-field enum, status/lifecycle, rulesApplied)
artifactPaths.actors: l4/actors/{module}Actors.defs.ts  (authorization roster: each actor + roleScope '{module}:{actorId}' for JWT, e.g. cafeFlow:managerOwner)
artifactPaths.operation: l4/operations/{operationId}.defs.ts  (GLOBAL; intent-level BFF contract: actor/entity/kind/reads/writes/rulesApplied + embedded story + canonical pageId/commandName/bffName)
artifactPaths.trace: l4/{module}/trace/NNN-agent.json (per-agent debug), l4/trace/checkpoint-{index}.json (frozen indices), l4/trace/behavior-health-report.json (kept across cleanup)

conventions.userStories: User stories/journeys are TRANSIENT input absorbed into each Workflow and Operation as an embedded `story` block (actor, goal, soThat, steps, outcome). No separate journeys artifact is produced, avoiding drift from duplicated story data.
conventions.ownerStatusForStage2: Each persisted workflow/operation carries TWO independent reconciler statuses — `statusFrontend` and `statusBackend` (each toCreate|toUpdate|toRemove|inProgress|done) — both seeded 'toCreate' on creation. agentChangeFrontend reads/writes statusFrontend; agentChangeBackend reads/writes statusBackend. Each reconciler processes owners whose own status != 'done' and flips it independently (no single-status ambiguity between the two stages). So Stage 1 leaves an explicit per-stage to-do list with progress tracking, not just the source model.
conventions.bffNamingSourceOfTruth: Each persisted Workflow carries canonical `pageId`. Each persisted Operation carries `pageId`, `commandName`, and `bffName = {moduleName}.{pageId}.{commandName}`. The pageId is the workflow that orchestrates the operation, or the operationId when no workflow owns it. Stage 2 l2/contracts and Stage 3 l1/controllers must reuse bffName rather than deriving route keys independently.
conventions.capabilitiesAsRealized: There is NO standalone capabilities artifact. Each Workflow carries `capabilities[]` and each Operation carries `capability` (capabilityId + title + priority), attached mechanically at save from the classification + final plan. So workflows/operations are the source of truth for which feature (and phase: now/soon/later) each behavior realizes. The full priority rationale also lives in module.defs.ts.designContext.decisions.
conventions.behaviorScope: agentClassifyBehavior classifies EVERY capability that is not priority 'never' (now AND soon AND later) — not just 'now'. This ensures user-requested key screens that landed as 'soon' (e.g. dashboards, AI summaries) still become operations and therefore get pages in Stage 2. Priority travels with the artifact so Stage 2 can phase the work.
conventions.workflowOperationLink: agentClassifyBehavior emits workflow.operationIds AND the corresponding operations; agentNs2WorkflowIndex deterministically fills any empty operationIds from the classification. A stateful workflow with zero operations is flagged as a warning.
conventions.idSpace: All entity references use canonical ONTOLOGY entity ids (the ontology map keys), qualified with the module when cross-module (e.g. cafeFlow:Order). NEVER aggregate/group names.
conventions.ontologyIsDataOnly: ontology.entities are persistent DATA nouns only (kind core/mdm/event/metric/supporting). NEVER use-cases/workflows/queries; no Uc*/verb-named ids (blueprint guard).
conventions.verification: No LLM critic/repair loop. Index agents run DETERMINISTIC reference-integrity checks (ids must resolve to canonical ontology ids; unresolved -> warnings, never a hard fail) and freeze a checkpoint. agentValidateBehaviorModel produces a non-blocking coverage/integrity report.
conventions.fanOutReads: Parallel fan-out children are pre-allocated, reused and DELETED after completion (mls.d.ts AgentIntentParallelSteps §7.1). Therefore consumers that run AFTER a fan-out read the SAVED l4 .defs.ts files (file-fallback), never the task payloads. See ns2Artifacts.read{OntologyEntities,WorkflowDefs,OperationDefs}.
conventions.finish: AUTOMATIC. There is no blocking final clarification. After agentValidateBehaviorModel, the final-resume step (an agent, not a clarification) writes l5/{module}/process.defs.ts with source refs, clears traces, CLEANS the task inputs/outputs, and completes the task. The Stage-1 summary is viewable any time via openStepView (rebuilt from the persisted report).
conventions.newTaskHandoff: Stage 2 (Experience) and Stage 3 (Backend) are NEW tasks reading only the frozen l4 artifacts. process.defs.ts records them as pending next-steps.

treeOverview: org-domain (container; blueprint -> review -> finalize -> per-entity ontology + mdm/horizontals/plugins refs)
org-behavior (container; classify into Workflow|Operation, then index+define workflows and operations)
org-handoff (container; validate the model, then auto-finish: freeze run, clean, complete)

steps.goal: Requirements container; renders the first clarification and the implementation decisions (shared widget-questions-for-clarification-102025). Module name answer kept RAW for the blueprint to interpret.
First small clarification (no architecture).
Behavior-level scope/signals (workflows/operations/mdm/horizontals/plugins/agents). Platform concerns out of scope.
Prioritized recommendations (now/soon/later/never). Behavior level only — never pages/tables.
User accepts/defers recommendations.
No-LLM container for the domain phase (ontology + rules).

Blueprint: module, actors, capabilities (each with behaviorHint workflow|operation|either), ontology map input (data nouns only), rules, relationships, behavior plan. CONFIRMS the module name (records 'module-name-final').
Non-blocking domain/coverage findings.
Freeze the domain plan and write the durable domain artifacts; spawn the per-entity ontology fan-out.
Per-entity canonical shape.
MDM domains as references; merges l5/project.json dependency on shared infra (102034) when used.
Horizontal module references (platform-provided concerns excluded).
External plugin references (reuse existing by brand).
No-LLM container for the behavior phase (workflows + operations) — the heart of Stage 1.

NEW. Classify EVERY non-'never' capability (now/soon/later) into a Workflow or Operation; emit workflow.operationIds + the operations they orchestrate; absorb the user stories as each owner's embedded `story`. Deterministic ref check + checkpoint.
Index workflows (executionMode, trigger, actors, entities, orchestrated operationIds). Deterministic ref check + checkpoint; spawns the definition fan-out.
Per-workflow definition; states aligned to the primary entity's lifecycle; pageId and capabilities[] attached mechanically.
NEW. Index operations (CRUD + query/view; dashboards as query/view). Deterministic ref check + checkpoint; spawns the definition fan-out.
NEW. Per-operation intent-level BFF contract (reads/writes/rulesApplied + story); capability plus pageId/commandName/bffName attached mechanically.
No-LLM container; opens validate + final-resume. SEPARATE from agentNewSolution2Final so only final-resume exposes openStepView (avoids a duplicate 'open summary' link).

NEW. Deterministic, NON-blocking coverage/integrity report read from the SAVED l4 files. NOTE: depends on the SAME upstream as org-handoff (NOT on org-handoff) — a child must never depend on its passive container or the container deadlocks (parent waits for children, child waits for parent).
AUTO-FINISH (no clarification): write l5/{module}/process.defs.ts with source refs, clear traces, clean task inputs/outputs, complete the task. Summary viewable via openStepView.

produces: l4/{module}/module.defs.ts (module meta + ontology index + relationships + approvedArtifacts)
l4/workflows/*.defs.ts (global workflows, embedded stories, canonical pageId)
l4/operations/*.defs.ts (global operations = intent-level BFF contract, embedded stories, canonical pageId/commandName/bffName)
